headline: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
description: CSRF forces authenticated users to execute unwanted actions on a web application. The attacker tricks the victim's browser into sending a request that the application treats as legitimate because it carries the user's session cookie. Prevention: anti-CSRF tokens, SameSite cookies, custom headers.
